item.title: Designing a GitOps Pipeline That Survives 3am
item.description:
The best compliment a deploy pipeline can get is silence. Nobody Slacks about it. Nobody “just kicks off the build.” It runs because someone merged a pull request, and the system quietly makes reality match the repo.

That’s the whole idea behind GitOps, and it’s less about tooling than it is about a single rule: the desired state of your system lives in git, and a machine — not a human — is responsible for closing the gap between git and production.

item.title: About
item.description:
I’m Jeff Bilder — a cloud architect and operations leader who builds the boring, dependable infrastructure that lets teams move fast without breaking things.

Over the years I’ve worn the hats of cloud architect, DevOps manager, infrastructure manager, and security operations manager — and the thread through all of them is the same: take systems that are sprawling, fragile, and scary to touch, and turn them into platforms that are automated, observable, and secure by default.
